display.purpose: Page titles — the largest heading in any view. The ceiling; never shouts.

body.purpose: Default running text and UI copy. Prose caps 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Buttons, form labels, nav items, table headers. Medium weight UI default.

mono.purpose: IDs, hashes, env keys, log lines, command output. Slashed zero + ss03.

shadows.name: lifted-surface
shadows.purpose: Graph nodes and lightly raised elements — a near-invisible seat, mostly a tonal ring.

shadows.name: floating-panel
shadows.purpose: Menus, popovers, dialogs, graph controls/minimap — clearly detached from canvas.

motion.name: transition-default
motion.purpose: State transitions on most interactive components (transition-all).

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: Tactile active-state nudge on buttons (suppressed on menu triggers).

motion.name: comet-spin
motion.purpose: Travelling light around a pending node's border (animated @property angle).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one solid-fill action per context. Signal Blue.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; gap: 6px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 0.625rem; background: oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376); color: #f5f5f0; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.18s ease; outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376 / 0.85); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { border-color: #a8a8a0; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376 / 0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Default-emphasis action sitting on the canvas.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; gap: 6px; border: 1px solid rgba(20,20,18,0.09); border-radius: 0.625rem; background: #fbfbfa; color: #141412; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.18s ease; outline: none; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: rgba(20,20,18,0.04); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { border-color: #a8a8a0; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376 / 0.5); } .ds-btn-outline:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Low-emphasis toolbar action; transparent until hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; gap: 6px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 0.625rem; background: transparent; color: #141412; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.18s ease; outline: none; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(20,20,18,0.04); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376 / 0.5); } .ds-btn-ghost:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Dangerous action as a tint, not a solid fill (The State-Tint Rule).
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; gap: 6px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 0.625rem; background: rgba(180,35,24,0.10); color: #b42318; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.18s ease; outline: none; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background: rgba(180,35,24,0.20); } .ds-btn-destructive:focus-visible { border-color: rgba(180,35,24,0.4); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(180,35,24,0.2); } .ds-btn-destructive:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard field; same focus signature as buttons.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 32px; width: 220px; padding: 4px 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(20,20,18,0.12); border-radius: 0.625rem; background: transparent; color: #141412; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; letter-spacing: -0.005em; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #7a7a74; } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #a8a8a0; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376 / 0.5); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat surface lifted off the canvas by a hairline ring, no shadow at rest.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; padding: 16px; border-radius: 0.625rem; background: #ffffff; color: #141412; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(20,20,18,0.10); font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.015em; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #7a7a74; } .ds-mono { font-family: 'Geist Mono Variable', ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, monospace; font-feature-settings: 'zero','ss03'; font-size: 0.8125rem; color: #141412; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Pill carrying the semantic state vocabulary (success shown).
components.html: Running
components.css: .ds-badge-success { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; height: 20px; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; background: rgba(31,122,63,0.12); color: #1f7a3f; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.005em; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar shares the canvas; active item uses Signal Blue, hover is a faint ink tint.
components.html: ProjectsLogsSettings
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; width: 200px; padding: 8px; background: #fbfbfa; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 0.625rem; color: #4a4a46; font-family: 'Geist Variable', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.005em; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.18s ease, color 0.18s ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgba(20,20,18,0.04); color: #141412; } .ds-nav-active, .ds-nav-active:hover { background: oklch(0.546 0.245 262.881 / 0.10); color: oklch(0.488 0.243 264.376); }

narrative.overview: otterdeploy is a precision tool that disappears into the hand. You feel the craft — the hairline rings, the slashed-zero monospace, the 1px nudge a button makes when you press it — but you never notice it, because nothing competes with the task. The interface earns trust by being calm, fast, and honest, not by being loud. The palette is a warm-neutral monochrome with a single OKLCH blue accent reserved for primary actions and current selection; state is carried by a tight semantic vocabulary that reads identically in light and dark. Typography is one family, Geist, in two cuts: variable sans for everything human-readable, mono for everything machine-readable. It explicitly rejects the cluttered self-host UI, the enterprise cloud console, and the generic AI-SaaS template.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-neutral monochrome surfaces; one OKLCH blue accent on ≤10% of any screen.
One type family (Geist sans + Geist mono), tight negative tracking, fixed rem scale — no fluid headings.
Flat by default: separation comes from hairline rings, not drop shadows.
Dense but orderly; legibility is never traded for compactness.
Semantic state color (success/warning/info/destructive) standardized and theme-symmetric.
First-class dark mode, held to the same contrast bar as light.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue appears on ≤10% of any given screen. It marks the primary action and the current selection — nothing else. Its rarity is what makes it read as 'do this.'

rules.name: The Warm-Black Rule
rules.body: No pure #000 and no pure #fff anywhere. Ink is #141412, canvas is #fbfbfa. The faint warmth is the whole point; pure values read as cheap.

rules.name: The State-Tint Rule
rules.body: Semantic color signals state through a low-opacity tint of its own hue plus same-hue text (bg-destructive/10 text-destructive), not a saturated fill. Reserve solid fills for the single most consequential action on a screen.

rules.name: The Two-Cuts Rule
rules.body: Sans for the human, mono for the machine. Anything a user could copy and paste into a terminal — an ID, a hash, a domain, a log line — is mono. Everything else is sans. No third font, ever.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: No clamp() headings in product UI. Users view at consistent DPI; a heading that shrinks inside a sidebar looks broken, not responsive.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Resting surfaces have no drop shadow. If a card needs a shadow to be distinguishable, the ring (ring-foreground/10) or background tone is wrong — fix that first. Shadows mean 'floating above the page,' not 'is a card.'

rules.name: The Ring-Not-Border Rule
rules.body: Card separation uses ring-1 ring-foreground/10 (a transparency of the ink), so it reads as a hairline regardless of theme. Audit test: if a divider looks grey rather than like a faint edge of the ink, it's a hardcoded grey — replace it.

dos: Do keep Signal Blue to ≤10% of any screen — primary action and current selection only (The One Voice Rule).
Do use #141412 for ink and #fbfbfa for canvas; never pure black or white (The Warm-Black Rule).
Do render state as a tint of its own hue plus same-hue text, reserving solid fills for the single most consequential action.
Do set every machine-readable string — IDs, hashes, domains, env keys, log lines — in Geist Mono with slashed zero (The Two-Cuts Rule).
Do separate surfaces with ring-1 ring-foreground/10; flat at rest, shadows only on floating layers (The Flat-By-Default Rule).
Do pair semantic color with an icon and label so state never depends on color alone.
Do use 150–250ms transitions, skeletons over spinners, and the active translate-y-px press; honor prefers-reduced-motion on every animation including the comet border.
Do design every state — loading, pending, degraded, error, empty, success — truthfully. Empty states teach the interface.
donts: Don't build the cluttered self-host UI — density without hierarchy, utilitarian noise. Density is fine; noise is not.
Don't build the enterprise cloud console — gray walls of nested config, endless tabs, no sense of state.
Don't reach for the generic AI-SaaS template: cream/sand backgrounds, gradient text, hero-metric blocks, identical icon-card grids, or a tracked-uppercase eyebrow above every section.
Don't introduce a second accent hue or a third font family. One blue, one family in two cuts.
Don't put drop shadows on resting cards, or use border-left/border-right >1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't nest a card inside a card.
Don't use clamp() / fluid headings in product UI (The Fixed-Scale Rule).
Don't ship fake, mock, or seeded data; unbuilt features show a 'Coming soon' placeholder, never invented content.
Don't let Muted Ink body text drop below 4.5:1 on its surface in either theme.
